page_content:
Formerly: ARHU329.

HISP - Historic Preservation

HISP200 The Everyday and the American Environment (3 Credits)

An introduction to the theories of the everyday within the context of

the American built environment. Focuses primarily on the American experience of underrepresented, minority, and/or immigrant communities; both historical and contemporary. Attempts to challenge what

is meant by American in describing the American everyday built environment.Jointly offered with HISP615.

HISP319 Special Topics in Historic Preservation (1-6 Credits) Students will explore technical aspects of preservation taught by practitioners whose expertise are of special benefit to undergraduate students.
